name: Baked Cod Chive Potato Puree & Caper Sauce - Scully on Spring
recipeIngredient: 2 tsp Coriander seeds, toasted and lightly crushed
1 small bunch of chives, chopped fine
1 cup Heavy cream (whole or skim milk can be used instead)
Salt and white pepper to taste
10 Ripe plum tomatoes, seeded and chopped
3 Garlic cloves, minced fine (up to 4 cloves)
1 small bunch of parsley, washed and roughly chopped
recipeInstructions:
For potatoes: Boil the potatoes until tender. Heat cream or milk in a saucepan. In a blender, blend the potatoes with butter, cream or milk, salt, and white pepper. Add chives before serving to retain color. Reserve.
For sauce: In a saucepan over medium-high heat, add olive oil. Add shallot, garlic, and plum tomatoes. Saute for two minutes, then add white wine. Reduce heat and let tomatoes and wine cook slowly until tomatoes fall apart. Finish sauce with capers, parsley, butter, salt, and pepper.
For the fish: Mix bread crumbs with coriander seeds. Season cod fish filets with salt and pepper. Dredge the top side of each fillet first in egg, then in bread crumb mixture. Heat an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat with vegetable oil. Place fish in the pan so that the breadcrumbs cook golden brown in the oil. Turn fish over, remove excess oil from the pan, and place in the oven to finish cooking (about five minutes depending on thickness of fillet).
To serve: Place a portion of chive potato puree in the middle of each plate. Place the cod fish filet over the potatoes and spoon caper-tomato sauce over the fish. Garnish with chives and parsley sprig.
Yield: Serves 4
